CREATE TABLE test_table (id INT, name VARCHAR(50));
UPDATE test_table SET name = 'Alice_updated' WHERE id = 1;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name) SELECT id + 2, CONCAT(name, '_new') FROM test_table WHERE id = 1;
SELECT COUNT(*) AS total FROM (SELECT DISTINCT name FROM test_table) AS unique_names;
CREATE TABLE another_table (id INT, foreign_id INT REFERENCES test_table(id));
UPDATE another_table SET foreign_id = 2 WHERE id = 1;
